Name: Key_leak_via_module_output
Description: The aes_128.key value may be exposed through the aes_128.out signal due to insufficient zeroization or side-channel/physical leakage paths. Monitor aes_128.out for unauthorized disclosure of the key material.

Name: State_leak_via_module_output
Description: Internal AES state (aes_128.state) may be exposed via aes_128.out due to insufficient clearing or side-channel leakage; check aes_128.out for unauthorized disclosure of internal state.

Name: Input_port_leak_via_output
Description: Values provided to aes_128.a1.in may remain or be leaked and become observable on aes_128.out due to insufficient zeroization or side-channel protections.

Name: Register_state_leak_via_output_and_debug
Description: aes_128.r1.state_in may be exposed through aes_128.out or debug-related paths due to uncleared debug information, improper debug access control, or side-channel leakage; monitor aes_128.out for such disclosures.

Name: Unauthorized_register_write_to_state_in
Description: An attacker able to control register-access inputs (aes_128.key or aes_128.state) or abuse debug interfaces can modify aes_128.r1.state_in and break integrity; validate access controls on register inputs.

Name: State_in_availability_via_output_path
Description: Fault injection (e.g., clock/voltage glitches) may impair aes_128.r1.state_in and cause availability failures observable on aes_128.out; monitor for fault-induced availability anomalies.

Name: Round_key_leak_via_output
Description: aes_128.r1.key may be exposed on aes_128.out due to insufficient zeroization, debug exposure, or side-channel leakage; inspect aes_128.out for key disclosure.

Name: State_out_dos_via_environment_or_glitch
Description: aes_128.r1.state_out availability may be disrupted by fault injection or environmental tampering, with effects observable on aes_128.out; monitor for anomalies indicating DoS.

Name: RF_state_in_leak_via_output_or_debug
Description: aes_128.rf.state_in may be disclosed via aes_128.out due to debug interface exposure, uncleared debug information, or side-channel leakage; check aes_128.out for unauthorized state disclosures.

Name: RF_unauthorized_register_write_or_misconnection
Description: Improper access control, misconnection, or debug misuse may allow modification of aes_128.rf.state_in via inputs such as aes_128.key or aes_128.state; validate register protections and correct block integration.

Name: PRELIM_A_7_REF_1
Description: Physical faults, environmental tampering, or incorrect integration can cause aes_128.rf.state_in to become unavailable or corrupted, producing incorrect or missing outputs observable on aes_128.out. Monitor aes_128.out for discrepancies that indicate loss of availability of the input state.

Name: PRELIM_C_8_REF_1
Description: Improper debug access or failure to zeroize the key register can result in aes_128.rf.key_in being exposed; an attacker observing aes_128.out may be able to infer or extract key material. Ensure aes_128.out does not leak key material and that key registers are zeroized and protected from debug access.

Name: PRELIM_A_9_REF_1
Description: Voltage/clock glitches or environmental tampering can corrupt aes_128.rf.state_out or interrupt correct output generation, leading to availability or incorrectness observable on aes_128.out. Detect such anomalies on aes_128.out to catch availability failures.

Name: PRELIM_C_10_REF_1
Description: Observable discrepancies or unprotected side-channel emissions can leak internal round state (aes_128.r1.t0.state) to an observer of aes_128.out; monitor and harden outputs to prevent leakage of intermediate state.
